summary:
Held 1 of today's 10 biggest UK/EU movers.

• 6 upside misses — RS1.L +15%, SHAW.L +7%, ASML.AS +7%, MKS.L +7%, WOSG.L +7%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 3 downside moves (AEP.L -21%, CWR.L -8%, CCR.L -5%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: AEP.L (-21.08%) — Anglo-Eastern Plantations filed RNS at 11:40 AM BST disclosing Indonesian government proposals for new CPO export restrictions, directly threatening AEP's Indonesia-derived palm oil revenues and triggering a sharp intraday re-rating. Why we passed: AEP Plantations has very thin LSE daily volume (~38m shares outstanding, low ADV) likely placing it below the bot's liquidity floor across all strategies. The catalyst was also a pure bearish intraday shock — no long-only strategy can profitably express a directional view on a >20% government-policy gap-down without sh

top_movers.catalyst: RS Group FY2026 full-year results (20 May): adj. operating profit £265M and revenue £2.88B both beat consensus; board simultaneously announced a £100M 12-month share buyback.
top_movers.miss_reason: Earnings-gate blocked pre-announcement positioning across all strategies. The ~8% open gap (600.5p → ~648p) then exceeded news-reactive's gap-entry ceiling, locking every strategy out of the further +6% continuation leg that ran to the 690p close.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-earnings continuation rule to news-reactive: if open gap ≥5% AND price holds above the first 5-min VWAP AND a capital-return event (buyback/special dividend) is simultaneously announced, permit a scaled entry within the first 30 min of open.

top_movers.catalyst: UBS upgraded ASML and raised price target from €1,600 to €1,900 (+19%), citing AI chip supply tightening and a semiconductor capex cycle extending to 2028; stock opened ~6% higher on the note.
top_movers.miss_reason: Analyst-upgrade catalyst landed pre-market; the ~6% open gap likely breached momentum-trader's max-gap-at-entry filter and pushed RSI above news-reactive's overbought ceiling, leaving all five strategies in observer mode while the move was already priced in.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a Tier-1-analyst-upgrade gate in news-reactive: if an overnight upgrade with PT raise ≥15% is detected and open gap is +3–8%, allow entry in the first 30 min with a tighter 1% hard stop, bypassing the RSI ceiling for this signal class only.

top_movers.catalyst: FY2026 results beat: adjusted PBT £671.4m vs £655m Shore Capital est.; food sales +7% YoY; higher full-year dividend (4.2p) proposed despite cyber-attack drag on H1 profit.
top_movers.miss_reason: Scheduled earnings release (pre-announced 20 May) triggers a pre-results exclusion gate in systematic strategies; the entire +6.64% move was an open-auction gap on a profit beat that required positioning before market open or at the first print — no prior-session technical signal existed to enter.
top_movers.suggestion: Earnings-gate should auto-release names where reported PBT beats consensus by ≥2% and the open-auction indicative price implies a gap ≥+3%; news-reactive can then enter within 15 min of LSE open on confirmed beats rather than staying gated all session.

top_movers.catalyst: FY26 trading update (14-May): record revenue £1.83B (+13% cc), US sales +24% to $1.24B, EBIT guidance raised to £152-155M; today's move is post-beat broker upgrade / institutional follow-through with no new discrete catalyst.
top_movers.miss_reason: News-reactive would have been eligible on 14-May's +15% gap; today has no fresh catalyst so the news-gate stays closed. Momentum-trader likely excluded it: RSI overbought after the gap and a recent-gap-cooldown filter bars new entries within the standard T+1-to-T+5 exclusion window.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a post-earnings continuation rule to momentum-trader: if a name gaps >10% on a results/trading-update day and then prints a new closing high on any T+1–T+5 session, suppress the gap-cooldown exclusion and allow a fresh entry sized at ≤50% normal position.

top_movers.catalyst: Mizuho raised STM price target to $68 from $56 (~21%), reinforcing the post-Q1 beat story ($3.10B rev, +23% YoY; Q2 guide $3.45B) and Starlink LEO supply deal (€3B cumulative 2026-28), lifting shares to a 52-week high.
top_movers.miss_reason: After a ~56% one-month rally RSI was deep in overbought territory, almost certainly tripping momentum-trader's entry filter. News-reactive likely closed its position post-earnings 3 weeks ago; today's move was an analyst PT-revision drift, not a fresh binary catalyst that would re-open a signal.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a tier-1 broker PT-revision trigger to news-reactive: when a universe name receives a ≥15% PT raise with a buy rating, allow a same-day entry even if RSI > 70, sized at 50% of normal to cap overbought-reversal risk.

top_movers.catalyst: Anglo-Eastern Plantations filed RNS at 11:40 AM BST disclosing Indonesian government proposals for new CPO export restrictions, directly threatening AEP's Indonesia-derived palm oil revenues and triggering a sharp intraday re-rating.
top_movers.miss_reason: AEP Plantations has very thin LSE daily volume (~38m shares outstanding, low ADV) likely placing it below the bot's liquidity floor across all strategies. The catalyst was also a pure bearish intraday shock — no long-only strategy can profitably express a directional view on a >20% government-policy gap-down without sh
top_movers.suggestion: Extend news-reactive's RNS watchlist to include LSE commodity-sector names above a minimum £200k ADV threshold with Indonesia/Malaysia exposure; pair with an intraday short-trigger rule on RNS keyword matches ('export levy', 'export restriction', 'export ban') if short capability is ever added.

top_movers.catalyst: Trump announced 19 May he called off a planned US military strike on Iran at Gulf allies' request, deflating crude's war-risk premium; WTI fell ~5% on eased Strait of Hormuz supply-disruption fears.
top_movers.miss_reason: Commodity-momentum held a long-USO signal built on the Iran-war scarcity premium; the reversal was a pre-market geopolitical headline creating a >3% opening gap, likely caught by the gap-exclusion filter. Long-only mandate meant the short side was never actionable.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a geopolitical de-escalation flag (news-sentiment feed): when triggered pre-market, suppress long commodity-ETF entries and allow short re-entry on gaps ≥2%, bypassing the gap-exclusion filter for geopolitical-reversal events only.

top_movers.catalyst: C&C Group FY2026 finals (18 May): group revenue -6%, op. profit €70.5M; distribution dragged by BBG/Budweiser exit & weak hospitality volumes; two-session post-results selloff continued into 20 May.
top_movers.miss_reason: Results RNS dropped 07:00 on 18 May — earnings gate likely held CCR off limits for ±2 days around announcement. By 20 May the primary news signal had already fired; news-reactive needs same-session entry and momentum lacked a prior-day directional setup.
top_movers.suggestion: Earnings-gate should release names with a post-results gap ≥3% within 30 min of LSE open on RNS day, letting news-reactive enter a short on the announcement session rather than sitting out the two-day post-results drift.
